data.title: U.S. tech hiring slows down as quality becomes a priority. Experis survey: Almost 50% of tech employers plan to hire, focusing on reskilling and flexible workforce.
data.description: Survey conducted by Experis reveals that approximately 50% of technology employers in the United States are intending to recruit new employees despite a noticeable slowdown in hiring at the start of 2026. However, the recruitment process is being hindered by ongoing skills shortages, prompting companies to invest in retraining programs and adopt flexible workforce methods. These initiatives aim to address the skills gap prevalent in the tech industry.

data.title: MetaVia Inc. announced pricing of $8.1 million underwritten public offering of shares of common stock on Jan. 15, 2026.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, MetaVia Inc. (Nasdaq: MTVA), a clinical-stage biotechnology company specializing in the treatment of cardiometabolic diseases, disclosed the details of its underwritten public offering of common stock. The announcement highlighted the pricing related to this offering.

data.title: SWBC and Certilytics team up to provide AI-driven health insights and benefit planning, enhancing strategies for clients.
data.description: In a recent collaboration, SWBC and Certilytics have joined forces to provide clients with proactive benefit planning strategies fueled by data-driven insights. This partnership aims to leverage the expertise of both companies to offer tailored solutions for optimizing benefits. The alliance brings together SWBC's industry experience with Certilytics' data analytics capabilities to deliver innovative strategies that enhance clients' planning processes. This collaboration is poised to revolutionize benefit planning by offering clients a unique approach backed by insightful data analysis and strategic planning.

data.title: Google founding board member Ram Shriram joins GridCARE's board of directors in Redwood City, CA; aiming to solve AI power issues alongside Stanford team.
data.description: A renowned figure from Silicon Valley has recently teamed up with Stanford University researchers to address a significant challenge in the field of artificial intelligence (AI): power consumption. The partnership aims to tackle the issue that has been identified as a major bottleneck for AI technology. The collaboration between the industry expert and the academic institution highlights the importance of finding solutions to improve efficiency in AI systems. This initiative has the potential to significantly impact the development and deployment of AI technologies in the future.

data.title: Moonshot and QumulusAI partner with Connected Nation IXP.us to deploy AI compute platform for underserved workloads.
data.description: A partnership has been formed between QAI Moon and Connected Nation IXP.us to introduce an AI compute platform in Lewisville, Texas. This platform will cater to next-generation inference workloads that are not adequately supported by hyperscalers.

data.title: Channelwill rebrands as CWILL, serves 30,000 eCommerce brands. Integrates 5 products into unified AI-driven platform.
data.description: In North Carolina, CWILL, formerly known as Channelwill, a prominent platform catering to 30,000 eCommerce brands worldwide, has consolidated its five products into a unified platform. This integration allows seamless customer data flow across tracking, returns, reviews, email, and support services. The core products have been renamed, with ParcelWILL (formerly ParcelPanel), TrustWILL (formerly Trustoo), SendWILL (formerly EcomSend), and SEOWILL (formerly SEOAnt). The rebrand also includes the introduction of Ch, streamlining services for clients.

data.title: Emplifi partners with Reddit for industry intel. Reddit API integrated in Emplifi to boost strategic execution.
data.description: Emplifi, a prominent customer engagement and social media marketing platform, has revealed a noteworthy partnership with Reddit, a hub for genuine online conversations. The integration of Reddit’s Enterprise API into the Emplifi system marks a strategic move, elevating Reddit beyond a mere listening tool to a vital analytical indicator for Emplifi Fuel. This collaboration aims to leverage Reddit's valuable data to enhance the platform's intelligent execution engine. This initiative is set to provide enhanced insights and decision-making capabilities based on high-intent community signals.

data.title: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley report strong multi-month rallies with mixed Q4 results and advances, as per Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Goldman Sachs, a leading financial firm, reported varied results on Thursday, while Morgan Stanley showed progress with a strong performance in Q4. Both companies are experiencing multi-month rallies following their latest reports.
IDF: In response to Hezbollah's repeated violations of the ceasefire understandings, the IDF is striking Hezbollah terror targets in several areas in Lebanon.

data.title: Curately.ai partners with LaborEdge to offer AI staffing innovation for healthcare agencies using NexusATS platform.
data.description: In Alpharetta, Georgia and Novi, Michigan, on January 15, 2026, Curately.ai and LaborEdge have entered into a strategic partnership to introduce advanced recruiting automation to healthcare staffing agencies through LaborEdge's NexusATS platform. This collaboration will provide agencies with access to cutting-edge technology for streamlining their recruitment processes and enhancing efficiency.

data.title: McDanel Advanced Materials and Richland Glass merge in a strategic move on Jan. 15, 2026, as Artemis portfolio companies.
data.description: In Beaver Falls, Pennsylvania, and Vineland, New Jersey, on January 15, 2026, McDanel Advanced Materials ("McDanel") and Richland Glass ("Richland"), both portfolio companies of Artemis, revealed their successful merger. This strategic move unites two companies specializing in material science, leveraging their synergies for mutual benefit and growth.

data.title: Matthew McConaughey takes a stand against AI misuse of his voice, armed with new trademarks and embracing tech cautiously.
data.description: McConaughey has obtained federal trademarks to protect against AI voice theft, equipping him with new tools. However, he is also open to embracing technology in his own way.

Trump administration is weighing the range of 5.2 to 5.6 billion gallons for bio-based diesel volumes, versus a proposed 5.6 billion gallons - Sources

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor's stock surge created $6 billion wealth for top investors like Ken Fisher, Laffont, and Sanders. TSM stock climbed 68% in the past year from $207 to above $347.
data.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd (NYSE: TSM) outperformed earnings expectations, leading to a surge in the stock to new record levels. The company demonstrated impressive growth with its stock rising approximately 68% in the past year, from $207 in mid-January 2025 to over $347 in premarket trading. This significant increase has prompted a reassessment of investor positions and highlighted the substantial wealth accumulation for long-term holders due to the AI rally. The details of this market development can be found in the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Seasats receives $24M funding from DoW to accelerate autonomous vessels fielding.
data.description: A well-known developer of long-endurance autonomous surface vessels, Seasats, disclosed on January 15, 2026, that it has been chosen by the Department of War (DoW) to be a recipient of an award as part of the initiative to speed up the procurement and deployment of innovative technologies.

data.title: Udemy and Indeed report on upskilling gaps, workers ahead of employers in skill building. Disconnect arises as employees prep for future, employers focused on current job openings.
data.description: In San Francisco, Udemy (Nasdaq: UDMY), a worldwide AI-driven skills enhancement platform, released new research in partnership with Indeed, the top job site globally, and a key job matching platform. The study, named “The Future-Proofing Instinct,” highlights a notable disparity between workers and companies. It shows that workers are actively enhancing their skills for tomorrow, yet numerous organizations are primarily concerned with filling current job vacancies. This emphasizes the evolving dynamic in the job market as employees strive to future-proof themselves while employers prioritize immediate hiring needs.
